summary: Updated tools/split_suggester.py so it no longer depends on parse_map/map.csv or optional .map input, instead reading names from config/GALE01/symbols.txt. The script was also adjusted to understand dtk-generated assembly syntax such as .text, .obj, and .fn directives, fix src path derivation from build/GALE01/asm paths, avoid crashes when float labels are missing, and print tracebacks on unexpected errors. The PR body explicitly states the goal: parse config/GALE01/symbols.txt and handle dtk-generated asm files.

key_files.role: only changed file; updated argument handling, symbol-name lookup, dtk asm parsing, output path handling, float literal guards, and exception reporting

what_changed: Removed the documented and implemented -m/--mapFile workflow for supplying an external .map file.
Removed import of parse_map and deleted parseMapCsv(), which previously generated and parsed build/map.csv.
Deleted parseMapFile(mapFilePath), which parsed function names from a .map file's .text section layout.

Added parseSymbolsTxt(), which reads config/GALE01/symbols.txt, strips comments after ';', extracts the symbol name before '=', extracts the address after ':', removes the leading 0x, and maps address strings to names.
Changed --noNames help text from disabling map file/map.csv lookup to disabling symbols.txt lookup.
Changed default name lookup in main() from parseMapCsv() to parseSymbolsTxt().
Changed assembly section tracking to recognize either .section directives or a bare .text directive.
Changed .sdata2 label parsing from colon-terminated labels to dtk-style .obj entries.
Changed .text function-start detection from colon-terminated labels to dtk-style .fn entries.
Changed generated source parent directory replacement from replacing \\asm\\ to replacing \\build\\GALE01\\asm\\ with \\src\\.
Guarded float literal emission so labels are only looked up in floatsData when present, avoiding unpacking None for labels that are not float data.
Added traceback.format_exc() output in the top-level exception handler before printing the error and exiting.
smart_moves: Switched function-name lookup to config/GALE01/symbols.txt, which is a project-local symbol source and avoids needing to regenerate build/map.csv via parse_map.
Removed the optional .map-file command-line path rather than preserving multiple name sources, simplifying tool behavior around one canonical symbol source.
Made parser changes at directive boundaries (.text, .obj, .fn), which directly targets the syntax differences in dtk-generated asm noted in the PR body.
Added membership checks before reading floatsData, preventing the split suggester from failing when uniqueLabels contains labels that are not recorded float literals.
Printed full tracebacks for unexpected failures, improving diagnostics for a developer-facing decomp tool.
decomp_lessons: When project assembly output format changes, decomp helper scripts often need syntax-level parser updates, not just path fixes.
Prefer canonical repository metadata such as config/GALE01/symbols.txt for symbol names when available, because generated map intermediates can add fragile tool dependencies.
Asm split/template generators should tolerate non-float labels in function reference lists; label collections are often broader than the data table a later phase wants to print.
Path rewriting in tools should match the actual build tree prefix, here build/GALE01/asm to src, rather than using a broad asm-to-src replacement.
For tools used during matching work, actionable traceback output is useful because malformed asm or symbol input can otherwise fail with little context.

naming_conventions.convention: symbols.txt names are treated as the preferred external function names
naming_conventions.evidence: parseSymbolsTxt() stores mapNames[addr] = name from config/GALE01/symbols.txt, and main() calls it unless --noNames is set

naming_conventions.convention: the --noNames option remains camel-cased in argparse as noNames
naming_conventions.evidence: existing -nn/--noNames flag was retained while its help text changed to reference symbols.txt

naming_conventions.convention: character source subfolders continue to use ft + capitalized character suffix naming
naming_conventions.evidence: existing charFolderName = 'ft' + filename[2].upper() + filename[3:] logic remains near the updated path replacement

assembly_or_matching_tactics: For dtk-generated asm, detect the text section from a line starting with .text as well as from .section.
For dtk-generated data labels in .sdata2, read label names from .obj directives instead of assuming bare labels ending in ':'.
For dtk-generated function starts in .text, read labels from .fn directives instead of assuming bare labels ending in ':'.
Use config/GALE01/symbols.txt to annotate generated function templates with known names, replacing older map.csv/.map parsing paths.
Before emitting float literal comments, check that a referenced label exists in floatsData to avoid crashing on unrelated labels.

review_feedback.comment: The provided PR context includes one issue comment but no review comments or reviews; no reviewer feedback was available in the excerpt.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching tools/split_suggester.py to see whether parseSymbolsTxt() needed more robust symbols.txt parsing.
Search for dtk-generated asm parser updates in other tools to reuse .obj/.fn/.text handling patterns.
Check whether config/GALE01/symbols.txt address formats are always compatible with line.split(':')[1].strip()[2:].
Look for split_suggester crash fixes involving floatsData or uniqueLabels after PR 1935.
